prompt:
A Collegiate Enrollment & Assessment Management System that includes course registration, academic review workflows, finance settlement, and governance controls for a university or training provider operating in an air‑gapped environment. Primary roles are Student, Instructor, Reviewer, Finance Clerk, and System Administrator, with Administrators defining organizations, terms, courses/sections, and registration rounds; Instructors managing rosters and initiating assessment/review cycles; Reviewers scoring assigned work; and Finance Clerks maintaining student financial ledgers. Client applications interact through domain APIs that support account sign-in, course discovery, eligibility checks, add/drop requests, waitlist placement, and retrieval of enrollment results and change history. The review and scoring workbench APIs support manual and rules-based reviewer assignment, blind and semi-blind review modes (identity visibility configurable per round), conflict-of-interest enforcement (e.g., reviewer cannot review a student from the same section or reporting line), multi-round scoring with customizable scoring forms, weighted aggregation across criteria, outlier handling (e.g., flag when a score deviates by >=2.0 points from the median on a 5-point scale), recheck workflows, and structured capture of comments with export. Notifications are supported as in-app messages only (offline constraint), with configurable triggers such as assignment posted, 72/24/2-hour deadline reminders, and grading completed, plus delivery/read tracking logs.

The backend runs as a single Docker-deployable Python service using FastAPI to expose resource-grouped interfaces (Authentication/Accounts, Courses/Registration, Reviews/Scoring, Finance/Settlement, Messaging, Admin/Governance, Integrations, and Data Quality), with SQLAlchemy for persistence into PostgreSQL. Authentication is strictly local username/password (no SMS/email codes and no third-party login); usernames may be student ID or employee ID, passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules, are salted+hashed, and lock after 5 failed attempts in 15 minutes with a 30-minute cooldown. Sessions/tokens expire after 8 hours idle and 24 hours absolute, with explicit revoke on deactivation. RBAC enforces menu/data permissions via org and class-scope grants, and every privileged write produces an immutable audit log entry (actor, action, before/after hash, timestamp) retained for 7 years. Course registration enforces time windows, prerequisites, and capacity with oversubscription prevention via transactional constraints; add/drop is idempotent using a required idempotency key (unique per actor+operation for 24 hours), and waitlists backfill automatically in priority order when seats open. Finance supports prepayment, deposit, and month-end billing as an internal ledger (USD), recording payment instruments as cash/check/internal transfer only; reconciliation compares ledger totals to imported local bank statement files, refunds create reversing entries, and arrears collection applies configurable late fees (e.g., 1.5% monthly after a 10-day grace period). Data quality controls include required-field coverage checks, range validation, uniqueness constraints, similarity-based deduplication (fingerprint + thresholded string similarity), quality scoring per record, and a quarantine queue for problematic writes. Open APIs support standardized local integration with SIS or question banks on the same network, including HMAC signature verification, per-client rate limiting (e.g., 120 requests/minute), and replay protection via nonce and timestamp.
